lines.en_lit: Were it not for the priestly vestments, there would not remain a remnant sarid or survivor from the haters of the Jewish people,

Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said the school Rabbi Shimon taught: garments that are woven in their completed form upon the loom, and a small part of them remains masridin What is Reish Lakish said: This is the needle-work

raises an objection to this Priestly vestments not be made through needle-work but woven work, as it is stated: “Woven work” Abaye said: is necessary only for, the sleeves. As it was taught A sleeve priestly vestments is woven separately and attached to the garment and reach as far as the palm of the hand.
Raḥava said Rav Yehuda said: Bezalel made three arks: A middle one of wood, nine an inner one of gold, eight outer one ten and a bit.

But wasn’t it taught eleven and a bit? This is not difficult: This is in accordance with the one who said the thickness was one handbreadth. That is in accordance with the one who said the thickness was not one handbreadth And what is bit? crown

Rabbi Yoḥanan said: There three crowns of the altar, and of the Ark, and of the table. of the altar Aaron was deserving and took it, of the table David was deserving and took it of the Ark it is still sitting and anyone who wishes to take come and take Perhaps you will say it is inferior the verse states “Through me kings will reign”
Rabbi Yoḥanan raised a contradiction: is written zar, but we read zeir, is deserving it becomes a crown [zeir] for him; is not deserving, will be a stranger [zara] to him

Rabbi Yoḥanan raised a contradiction: It is written: “And you shall make for yourself a wooden Ark” and it is written: “And they shall make an Ark of acacia wood” from here to a Torah scholar that the members of his town should perform his work for him
“From within and from without you shall cover it” Rava said: any Torah scholar whose inside is not like his outside, is not a Torah scholar.
Abaye said, and some say Rabba bar Ulla called loathsome, as it is stated: “What then of one loathsome and foul, man who drinks iniquity like water”

Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said Rabbi Yonatan said: What is which is written: “Why is there a price in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, as he has no heart?” Woe to them, haters of Torah scholars, who immerse themselves in Torah and have no fear of Heaven.
Rabbi Yannai declared Pity who has no courtyard but makes a gate for his courtyard.
Rava said to the Sages I beg of you, do not inherit Gehenna twice.

Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi said: What is which is written: “And this is the Torah which Moses put sam is deserving, becomes a potion [sam] of life for him. is not deserving, becomes a potion of death for him. And this is what Rava said: For one who is skillful a potion of life; for one who is not skillful it is a potion of death.

Rabbi Shmuel bar Naḥmani said Rabbi Yonatan raised a contradiction: It was written: “The precepts of the Lord are upright, gladdening the heart” “The word of the Lord is refining” is deserving, gladdens him; is not deserving, refines him. Reish Lakish said: emerges from that verse itself: is deserving, refines him for life; is not deserving, it refines him for death.
“Fear of the Lord is pure, it stands forever” Rabbi Ḥanina said: This one who studies Torah in purity; What is this; One marries a woman and afterward studies Torah.
“The testimony of God is faithful” Rabbi Ḥiyya bar Abba said: is faithful to testify about those who study it
“work of an embroiderer” “work of a designer” Rabbi Elazar said: They embroidered the place where they had designed.
taught in the name of Rabbi Neḥemya: an embroiderer” needlework, therefore one face. a designer” woven work, therefore two faces.

in these be consulted for Urim VeTummim. When Rav Dimi came said: garments in which the High Priest serves priest anointed for war serves. As it is stated: “And the sacred garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him” to the one who comes after him in greatness,

Rav Adda bar Ahava raised an objection, and some say it unattributed: might the son of the priest anointed for war serves in his place, in the same way that the son of a High Priest serves in his place
